home.title: Overview
home.description: Project sections. Full service catalog — "All services" in the sidebar.

placeholder.scaffoldReady: Scaffold is ready — content will appear in the next pass.

list.title: Virtual machines
list.deleteDescription: This action is irreversible. VM resources and associated disks will be released.

empty.title: No virtual machines yet
empty.description: Create your first VM — Plexor brings it up on the chosen runtime.
empty.noResultsDescription: Try adjusting the filters or reset them.

new.title: Create virtual machine
new.description: VM-specific settings. Node and cluster are selected below.
new.nodeDescription: Only {{badge}} nodes accept new VMs. Pending/draining/offline are filtered out.
new.paramsDescription: Name, image, network and SSH key.
new.name: Name
new.nameDescription: Used as hostname. Lowercase letters, digits and hyphens.

noCluster.title: Plexor control plane is not registered
noCluster.description: To create VMs, you first need to install Plexor on a server. The installation sets up the control plane and registers it in the UI.

noReady.title: No ready nodes
noReady.description: Connect Plexor.NodeAgent to the cluster via a join token. After a heartbeat (≤ 2 min) the node appears here and you can create VMs.

form.pageDescription: Full KVM configuration — placement, hypervisor, storage and network are all yours to set.
form.placementDescription: Which node hosts the VM. Only ready nodes accept new VMs.
form.nodeHelp: Self-hosted: the VM runs on a specific node, not an abstract zone.
form.machineTypeHelp: q35 exposes modern PCIe; i440fx for legacy guests.
form.firmwareHelp: UEFI (OVMF) for modern images; SeaBIOS for legacy BIOS boot.
form.cpuTypeHelp: `host` passes the physical CPU through (fastest; no live-migration across dissimilar CPUs).
form.imageDescription: Boot disk template. From the Images catalog.
form.computeDescription: CPU and memory. Sizes are exact (down to MiB) — self-hosted, not fixed cloud «flavors».
form.memoryHelp: Exact size, down to the MiB. Limited by the node's free memory.
form.computeAdvanced: Advanced · CPU type, NUMA, memory ballooning
form.numaHelp: Expose host NUMA topology to the guest — for large multi-socket VMs.
form.ballooningHelp: Let the host reclaim unused guest memory down to the minimum below.
form.minMemoryHelp: Balloon floor — the guest keeps at least this much.
form.storageDescription: Boot disk + extra volumes. Pools come from the node's backends
form.bootDiskPoolHelp: Storage backend the disk lives on — offered by the selected node (Ceph, LVM-Thin, ZFS…).
form.bootDiskSizeHelp: Down to the MiB. Must be ≥ the image's minimum disk.
form.busHelp: VirtIO SCSI is the modern default; SATA for compatibility.
form.storageAdvanced: Advanced · Cache, SSD emulation, discard, IO thread, extra disks
form.cacheModeHelp: `none` is safest with Ceph/ZFS; `writeback` is fastest but needs a UPS.
form.ssdEmulationHelp: Report the disk as non-rotational to the guest.
form.discardTrimHelp: Pass guest TRIM through so freed blocks return to the pool (thin provisioning).
form.ioThreadHelp: Dedicated IO thread per disk — better throughput on VirtIO.
form.extraDisksDescription: Additional data volumes attached at boot.
form.ipAddressHelp: DHCP from the subnet, or pin a static address + gateway.
form.networkAdvanced: Advanced · NIC model, VLAN, firewall, rate limit
form.nicModelHelp: VirtIO is fastest; e1000/rtl8139 for guests without VirtIO drivers.
form.vlanTagHelp: 802.1Q tag on the port. Empty = untagged.
form.firewallHelp: Attach the node's firewall to this NIC.
form.accessDescription: Name, first user, keys and provisioning.
form.authenticationHelp: An SSH key is recommended; a password is optional.
form.userDataHelp: Extra cloud-init YAML merged on first boot (packages, run-cmd…).
form.guestAgentHelp: Enables graceful shutdown, IP reporting and fs-freeze snapshots. The image must ship qemu-guest-agent.
form.protectionHelp: Blocks accidental deletion until turned off.
form.labelsHelp: key=value pairs for grouping and search.

empty.title: No Kubernetes clusters yet
empty.description: Deploy managed K3s on your Plexor fleet — control plane, node pools, networking.
empty.noResultsDescription: Try adjusting the filters or reset them.

new.title: Create Kubernetes cluster
new.description: Managed K3s on the Plexor fleet — control plane, node pools, networking and storage are all yours to set.

noCluster.title: Plexor control plane is not registered
noCluster.description: To create a Kubernetes cluster, you first need to install Plexor on a server. The installation sets up the control plane and registers it in the UI.

noReady.title: No ready nodes
noReady.description: Connect Plexor.NodeAgent to the cluster with a join token. After a heartbeat (≤ 2 min) the node appears here and you can host a Kubernetes cluster on it.

form.basicsDescription: Name, Kubernetes version, and which Plexor fleet hosts it.
form.nameHelp: Cluster name. Lowercase letters, digits and hyphens.
form.fleetHelp: Which fleet hosts the cluster — nodes are provisioned from its ready hosts.
form.controlPlaneDescription: How the K3s API server and datastore are laid out.
form.modeHelp: HA runs 3 control-plane nodes with embedded etcd quorum.
form.datastoreHelp: Embedded etcd (self-managed quorum) or an external SQL datastore.
form.nodePlacementHelp: Let the scheduler place control-plane nodes automatically, or pin them to specific hosts.
form.nodePoolsDescription: Groups of identical nodes that span the fleet. Sizes are exact — self-hosted, not fixed cloud «flavors».
form.networkingDescription: CNI, ingress and the service load balancer. CIDRs live under «Advanced».
form.cniHelp: Pod network plugin. Flannel is the K3s default; Cilium/Calico for policy + eBPF.
form.ingressHelp: Bundled ingress controller for HTTP routing.
form.loadBalancerHelp: How LoadBalancer Services get an address. ServiceLB (klipper) is the K3s default.
form.podCidrHelp: Address range pods are allocated from.
form.serviceCidrHelp: Address range ClusterIP Services are allocated from.
form.storageDescription: Default StorageClass for dynamic volumes. Backends come from the fleet's providers.
form.storageClassHelp: Only backends the chosen fleet actually runs are offered.
form.metricsServerHelp: Resource metrics API for `kubectl top` and the HPA.
form.certManagerHelp: Automated TLS certificate issuance and renewal.
form.monitoringHelp: Prometheus + Grafana stack for metrics and dashboards.
form.dashboardHelp: Web UI for browsing and managing cluster workloads.
form.autoUpgradeHelp: Follow the K3s release channel and upgrade nodes automatically.
form.protectionHelp: Blocks accidental deletion until turned off.
form.labelsHelp: key=value pairs for grouping and search.

empty.title: No containers yet
empty.description: Deploy LXC containers on ready nodes with an LXD/LXC runtime provider.
empty.noResultsDescription: Try adjusting the filters or reset them.

noCluster.title: Plexor control plane is not registered
noCluster.description: To create containers, you first need to install Plexor on a server. The installation sets up the control plane and registers it in the UI.

noReady.title: No LXC-capable nodes
noReady.description: Containers need a ready node with an LXD/LXC runtime provider. Connect an LXD-capable node (select the lxd provider at install), then it appears here.

form.pageDescription: Full LXC configuration — placement, template, resources, storage and network are all yours to set.

placement.title: Placement
placement.description: Which node hosts the container. Only ready nodes with an LXD/LXC runtime are listed.
placement.nodeHelp: Self-hosted: the container runs on a specific node, not an abstract zone.

template.title: Template
template.description: Root filesystem template the container is built from.
template.unprivilegedHelp: Maps container UIDs to an unprivileged host range — safer isolation. Turn off only for workloads that need host-level privileges.

compute.title: Compute
compute.description: CPU and memory. Sizes are exact (down to MiB) — self-hosted, not fixed cloud «flavors».
compute.coresHelp: CPU cores the container may use.
compute.memoryHelp: Exact size, down to the MiB. Limited by the node's free memory.
compute.cpuLimitHelp: Hard ceiling in cores (fractional allowed via host cgroup). 0 = no limit.
compute.cpuUnitsHelp: Relative CPU weight vs other containers under contention (default 1024).

rootfs.title: Root filesystem
rootfs.description: Rootfs + extra mount points. Pools come from the node's backends.
rootfs.descriptionNoNode: Rootfs + extra mount points. Pools come from the node's backends (select a node first).
rootfs.poolHelp: Backend the rootfs lives on — offered by the selected node (Ceph, LVM-Thin, ZFS…).
rootfs.mountsDescription: Extra data volumes mounted into the container.

network.title: Network
network.description: veth interface into a VPC + addressing.
network.ipHelp: DHCP from the subnet, or pin a static address + gateway.
network.advanced: Advanced · Interface, VLAN, firewall, rate limit
network.vlanHelp: 802.1Q tag on the veth. Empty = untagged.
network.firewallHelp: Attach the node's firewall to this interface.

features.title: Features
features.description: Container capabilities beyond the safe default set.
features.keyctlHelp: Allow the keyring syscalls — needed by some runtimes (e.g. systemd, Docker).
features.fuseHelp: Allow userspace filesystems inside the container.

access.title: Access
access.nameHelp: Used as the hostname. Lowercase letters, digits and hyphens.
access.authHelp: An SSH key is recommended; a password is optional.
access.dnsHelp: Space-separated resolvers pushed to the container.

options.title: Options
options.protectionHelp: Blocks accidental deletion until turned off.
options.labelsHelp: key=value pairs for grouping and search.

empty.title: No registered clusters
empty.description: Install Plexor on your server via {{code}} or an ISO image, then register the control plane here.

detail.notFoundDescription: Cluster with id {{id}} does not exist or was deleted.
detail.selfHelpDescription: Plexor self-hosted — you install control-plane and nodes on your own hardware.
detail.tokensDescription: Generated here, copied to the node, used once.
detail.tokensExpireNote: Tokens expire — after joining, the node stays in the Nodes list.
detail.nodesDescription: Plexor.NodeAgent instances that joined this cluster.
detail.noNodes: No nodes yet. Add one with a join token.
detail.noTokens: No join tokens yet. Create one to add a node.

addNode.title: Add a node to the cluster
addNode.descriptionBefore: Generate a join token. Install the Plexor ISO on the target machine, then run the command
addNode.descriptionAfter: with this token. The node appears in the list after the first heartbeat (≤ 2 min).
addNode.labelHint: For identification only. Does not affect the node itself.
addNode.roleHint: Control-plane only if you are building a fault-tolerant control HA.
addNode.ttlHint: Once it expires, the token becomes invalid and the node will not join.
addNode.tokenValidUntil: Valid until {{date}}. After joining, the token stays in the list but cannot be reused.
addNode.commandHint: Run on the machine booted from the Plexor ISO (see below).

steps.title: Steps on the node
steps.boot: Boot the target server from this ISO.
steps.appear: After booting, the node appears in the list
steps.runBefore: Run the command above. The node appears as

images.title: Images
images.description: OS images and custom snapshots for VM provisioning.

empty.title: No images yet
empty.description: An image is a disk template that a VM is created from. Upload your own (qcow2/raw) or use public distributions.

networks.title: Networks and VPC
networks.description: VPC, subnets and security groups for the project.

audit.title: Audit log
audit.description: History of user and system actions in the project.

billing.title: Billing
billing.description: Resource billing and consumption by project.

form.description: The engine is decoupled from the runtime — choose where to bring it up.
form.engineCardDescription: Preinstalled managed engine. Plexor takes over the ready-made artifact.
form.resourcesCardDescription: A preset or an exact configuration matched to the node's resources.
form.cpuHelp: Number of cores. Limited by the runtime node's available resources.
form.ramHelp: Exact size, down to the MiB (like in Proxmox). Limited by the runtime node's available memory.
form.diskHelp: Exact size, down to the MiB — how the volume is physically partitioned. Limited by the node's capacity.
form.nameHelp: Latin letters, digits, and hyphens. Used as the cluster name and in DNS.
form.backupsHelp: Scheduled backups (WAL/snapshots) retained on a schedule.
form.backupsUnsupported: This engine has no built-in backups yet.
form.retentionHelp: How long automatic backups are kept, in days.
form.pwGenerateHint: A strong password will be generated and shown once after creation.
form.labelsHelp: key=value pairs for grouping and search.
form.runtimeDescription: All runtimes are shown; unavailable ones list the reason. Default is direct.

preferences.title: Visual settings
preferences.description: Theme, accent color and text size. Saved in this browser.
